bloodDonation.type: Double Reds (Power Red) - 2 units RBCs per donation

clinicalWorkupAsks.nextPhysicianVisit: Request the following labs together to cover multiple coaching questions:

required.reason: Monitor iron balance given Double Reds donation 2-3x/year + 65mg daily iron supplementation. Target 30-200 ng/mL. Below 30 = deficient despite supplement; above 200 = overloaded, reduce dose.
Complete iron workup alongside ferritin. Detects anemia or overload context.

recommended.reason: Baseline given current 5g/day psyllium (under the 10-15g dose needed for measurable LDL effect). If LDL elevated, bump psyllium to 10g/day for 8-12 weeks and recheck. Also relevant for fish oil dose review (currently 980mg EPA+DHA, sub-therapeutic for lipid effects).
One-time baseline. San Diego + outdoor play = likely sufficient, but a data point is cheap. Target 30-60 ng/mL.

recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.reason: Hyperthyroidism is one of the most common drivers of elevated SHBG. Jul 2025 panel showed SHBG 83 (upper limit 50). Rule out thyroid driver before attributing high SHBG to genetic/idiopathic.
Liver synthesizes SHBG. Elevated enzymes or altered liver function can increase SHBG. Rule out liver-driven elevation.
Low-insulin states elevate SHBG. Given mixed metabolic picture, worth characterizing insulin sensitivity.

recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.test: Repeat T panel in 3-6 months with free T + SHBG + Estradiol + Prolactin
recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.reason: Confirm the elevated-SHBG pattern is persistent, not a one-time reading. Same collection protocol (morning, fasted, pre-exercise, not within 2-3 weeks of Power Red donation).

testosteroneHistory.notes: Reference range 40s: 264-916 ng/dL. Within normal. Collection time unknown - if drawn afternoon, AM equivalent would have been higher since T is ~50% lower PM vs AM.
Optimal morning collection. Above age-40 average (~500-600 ng/dL). Free T and SHBG not drawn - limited interpretation without them (see 2025-07 panel showing high-SHBG pattern).

testosteroneHistory.freeTestosteroneFlag: low-normal (at floor, 3.4 pg/mL above deficiency threshold)
testosteroneHistory.notes: KEY FINDING: elevated SHBG (83 vs upper limit 50) is pulling total T out of circulation. Total T 811 is upper-normal but free/bioavailable T are at the BOTTOM of range. This is a 'functionally low-T presentation' despite robust total T. Technically does not meet Endocrine Society 2018 hypogonadism criteria (free T above floor, no reported symptoms). Borderline high estradiol tracks with high total T (aromatization). Prolactin normal rules out pituitary cause. Next step: understand SHBG driver - thyroid (TSH + free T4), liver (AST/ALT/bilirubin), metabolic (fasting insulin / HbA1c). Source: Quest Diagnostics ZD478125F Men's Hormone Panel Expanded, ordering physician MARKS, Lianne, PWN Health.

nutritionGoals.primary: Steady fat loss preserving lean mass, strength, and game performance
nutritionGoals.rationale: Apr 20 body-comp overview showed +4.7 lbs fat / +1.9 lbs lean over 6 weeks. Initial 2-day/week deficit-at-BMR approach (set Apr 20 AM) was recalibrated later that day based on Helms 2014 and Aragon 2017 ISSN evidence: (1) rest-day target at BMR was more aggressive than needed, (2) intermittent restriction is not superior to daily small deficits per ISSN 2017, (3) weekly deficit was below the Helms 0.5%/wk floor anyway. Revised to moderate-deficit across 3 non-heavy-training days.

goals.name: Moderate-deficit days
goals.weeklyDeficitEstimate: ~900-2,100 cal/week = ~0.3-0.6 lb/week fat loss (within Helms 2014 0.5-1%/week lean-preserving range)
goals.rationale: Mon = post-Sunday-game recovery (no training). Thu = upper body lifting only (no lower-body demand). Sat = pre-Sunday-game prep (no training). Wed (heavy TB DL) and game days (Tue, Sun) stay at maintenance to fuel performance. Daily deficit of 300-500 cal below TDEE preserves lean mass per Helms 2014.
goals.evidence: Helms et al. 2014, JISSN (PMID 24864135): 0.5-1% BW/week loss rate for lean-mass preservation in resistance-trained athletes. Aragon et al. 2017 ISSN position stand (PMID 28630601): intermittent restriction not superior to daily small-deficit approach.

goals.name: Restaurant meal cap
goals.rationale: Hall 2019 ward study (Cell Metab) showed ultra-processed/restaurant food environments drive ~+500 cal/day spontaneous intake vs matched whole-food diets. Current pattern: 5-7 restaurant meals/week consistently drives 3,000+ cal days. Capping at 3 fixes the weekly caloric creep without daily macro tracking.

supersededGoals.name: Deficit rest days (original Apr 20 AM)
supersededGoals.supersededReason: Target was at BMR (~1,990 cal) which was more aggressive than needed. Math using rest-day TDEE (~2,400) showed actual deficit was only 300-400 cal per day = 600-800 cal/week, below the Helms 0.5%/week floor anyway. Revised to gentler 3-day approach.

dailySupplements.name: One A Day Men's multivitamin
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Weak (athletes with adequate diet); see knowledge/supplements.md

dailySupplements.name: Fish oil
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Moderate for athletes; dose may be sub-therapeutic for tendon/recovery effects (2-3g EPA+DHA typical)

dailySupplements.name: Iron + vitamin C
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: CLINICALLY APPROPRIATE for Double Reds donor (2-3x/year). Red Cross recommends 18-38mg elemental for 120 days post-Power Red; his ~940-1,410mg/year iron loss justifies chronic supplementation. Dose is slightly above official 18-38mg range but within typical OTC tablet strength. Annual ferritin check warranted to confirm iron balance.

dailySupplements.name: Magnesium glycinate
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Moderate; appropriate dose and form for sleep/recovery

dailySupplements.name: Psyllium husk powder
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Strong for cholesterol, moderate for glycemic control and satiety. Jovanovski 2018 meta-analysis (Am J Clin Nutr, PMID 30239559): psyllium reduces LDL cholesterol 5-10% at 7-15g/day. 5g/day is modest; could benefit from 2-3x daily dosing or bump to 10g for full LDL effect per FDA qualified health claim threshold.

full (with granola).ingredients: 1 cup whole milk, 1 cup nonfat Greek yogurt, 2 scoops ON Gold Standard whey, 2 tbsp PB Fit, 2/3 cup NV Protein Granola, 5g creatine, 5g psyllium husk powder

without granola (with yogurt).ingredients: 1 cup whole milk, 1 cup nonfat Greek yogurt, 2 scoops ON Gold Standard whey, 2 tbsp PB Fit, 5g creatine, 5g psyllium husk powder

without granola or yogurt.ingredients: 1 cup whole milk, 2 scoops ON Gold Standard whey, 2 tbsp PB Fit, 5g creatine, 5g psyllium husk powder

activityLog.notes: Whole-foot compression/squeezing pain (both feet). Likely sock fit issue — not structural. Try non-compression cushioned socks + check lacing.
PM session. Double game day complete.

activityLog.details: Bike 12 min, seated calf raise HSR 3×10 (BW/25/50 lbs), 90/90 stretches, massage gun
activityLog.notes: Tired + lower back tightness AM. Light arch pain on bike when on balls of feet — resolved by moving to mid-foot pedal position. Calf raises felt good ramping up.
Initial left achilles pain pre-game, resolved with warm-up + single-leg calf raises. No pain during game. Thorlo socks helped with foot compression issues from 3/8.
First lifting session of the program. All exercises as prescribed. GHR chosen over Nordic.
Second lifting session. Used assist machine for pull-ups (60 lbs off BW). DB bench warm-up at 45, working sets at 60.
Replaced Friday routine. Good paddling volume — shoulders, arms, and core work. Cross-training session.
Sunday AM session. Week 2 opener.
Sunday PM session. Double game day.
Tuesday PM session. No achilles pain. Longest game session so far in the program.
Week 2 lower body. All lifts progressed from Week 1. GHR fatigued on last set (3 reps vs 5).
Week 2 upper body. All lifts progressed from Week 1. Switched from assisted pull-ups to lat pulldown, ramped to 135. DB bench all working sets at 65 (was 60). DB OH press up to 25 (was 20).
Week 2 Friday session. First Friday lower moderate session of the program. Goblet squat at 25 lbs, hip thrust on machine at 50 lbs.
Week 4 (deload week) Sunday AM basketball. Shorter session, moderate intensity. No hockey this week.
Limited hotel gym. Upgraded from bodyweight hotel plan to weighted exercises. Added leg curls for hamstring volume. Single-leg RDLs jumped to 30 lb DBs (was 25 in Week 2).

activityLog.activity: Upper Body + Core — Hotel Gym (Deload Week 4)
activityLog.notes: Swapped planned lower body for upper body. Had access to dumbbells and cable machine — significant upgrade over bodyweight plan. DB press at 50 lbs (down from 65 in Week 2 — appropriate deload range). Good core work with anti-rotation and rotational exercises.

activityLog.activity: Combined Deload — Full Body (Fri Deload Week 4)
activityLog.notes: Gym deload session — first barbell work since Week 2 (Mar 20, two weeks ago). TB DL at 195 (~85% of 230). DB bench 55 (~85% of 65). HSR calf raises at full load per protocol. Executed as prescribed in modified week plan.
Week 5 Day 1 — re-baseline session. TB DL back to 225 (Week 2 weight) after 20-day gap. Back squat unavailable, substituted slanted leg press sled (90→180 lbs). HSR maintained at 140.

Week 5 Day 2 — first barbell bench press session. BB bench at 135 as baseline. Lat pulldown matched at 135 (up from 115 in deload). Subbed bent over barbell row for chest-supported row. DB bench and OHP as prescribed. Full core circuit.

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.notes: Warm-up sets at 45 and 95, working sets at 135

activityLog.notes: Week 5 Day 3 — pulled Thursday's Lower Body #2 forward to Wednesday. Back squat at 135 (matching Monday's working weight). All accessories as prescribed. Standing calf raise HSR for achilles protocol.
Week 5 Day 4 — Friday lower moderate session. Goblet squat at 30 (top of prescribed range). Hip thrust at 45. HSR maintained. Full mobility circuit.
Week 6 Day 1 — first game back after 2-week break (no games since Mar 29). Games resume on normal schedule.
Week 6 — PM session. Double game day complete (basketball AM + hockey PM).
Week 6 — Tuesday evening session. Shorter play time than usual.
Week 6 Day 1 — Wed session done on Thursday. Progression on all lifts: TB DL 225→230, squat 135→140, BSS 15→20 DBs, SLRDL 25→30 DBs, HSR 140→145. BSS and SLRDL approaching RPE 7+.

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.notes: Second BB bench session, +5 lbs from Apr 7

activityLog.notes: Week 6 Day 2 — Thu upper body pushed to Fri. Closes out Week 6 upper volume (chest 4, back 8, shoulders 6, core 9). Progression across all primary lifts: BB bench 135→140 (benchPress baseline updates), lat pulldown 135→140, bent row 135→140 with added set, DB OHP 25→30. Core circuit RX'd.

Week 7 Day 1 — single-game Sunday per modifiedWeeks.7 (no hockey this week). Indoor court (moderate sweat vs outdoor sun exposure). AM achilles stiffness 1-2/10 (within <15 min typical baseline per Silbernagel framework). During play after warmup 0-1/10. Sleep 8h, at upper end of target. All pain-monitoring signals green.

Non-programmed activity: digging holes + weeding (standing and squatting). Real lower-body load from shoveling/hip-hinge + 2 hours of intermittent unloaded squats. Functionally closer to a loaded carry + low-intensity squat session than passive yardwork. Estimated 500-700 cal expenditure given lower-body involvement. Adds meaningful posterior-chain and quad fatigue on top of morning basketball eccentric load.

Week 7 Day 3 Tue PM game. Shorter than typical 40-50 min session - reduced total sport load this week. Combined with single-game Sunday, total Week 7 sport minutes are about half a normal week. Good for Wed TB DL recovery.

scienceReviews.findings: HSR compliance low (33-67%), chest/back volume below 6-set minimum, body comp trending wrong direction, only 2 full training weeks in 4
scienceReviews.changes: Extended Phase 1 to Week 8, BB bench weekly + DB accessory, added chest-supported row, added sleep/protein tracking, travel-proof HSR protocol recommended

scienceReviews.findings: HSR compliance recovered to 3/3 in Week 5 (100%), 1/3 in Week 6 to date. Chest 7 sets and back 7 sets in Week 5, both above the 6-set minimum. Shoulders and core at the 6-set floor. Protein 181 g/day Week 5 and 164 g/day Week 6, both inside 160-220 target. Calorie intake 1,997 Week 5 and 2,392 Week 6, near maintenance given reduced post-gap TDEE. Body composition flat at 221-223 lbs / 23.4-23.7 percent over 12 days. TB DL stalled at 230x5 for 5 real training weeks (travel plus deload absorbed the middle). Back Squat 135 to 140 on pace after Week 5 intro. BB Bench has one session at 135 on Apr 7, second session still pending. Accessories progressing aggressively: BSS 10 to 20 DBs, SLRDL 25 to 30 DBs, RPE 7+. No sleepHours or achillesPain values logged in Week 5 or Week 6, leaving a data gap for the 12-week HSR adaptation check. Week 6 missing its Thursday upper body session.
scienceReviews.changes: Recommend fitting upper body session Fri or Sat to close Week 6 volume, adding two more HSR sessions before Saturday, microloading TB DL to 232.5 or adding a fifth set at 230 on next Monday to break the stall, resuming sleepHours and achillesPain capture per CLAUDE.md format, and trimming roughly 300 cal/day if fat loss remains a priority. No program structure changes; Phase 1 to 2 transition stays on Week 9 and 12-month targets will be reassessed 2-3 weeks into Phase 2.

Final: 278g/3,110cal.

AM weigh-in 225.8/24.4%. +4.0 lbs and +1.0 pt BF vs Apr 15 - acute spike from high-sodium week + glycogen repletion post-Apr 17 training + high-carb dinner. Not a real body-comp change. Breakfast: Nómade breakfast burrito (~700cal/32g estimate). Lunch: 2 boneless skinless chicken thighs (~340cal/50g), 1.5 russet potatoes with olive oil (~366cal/6.5g), 1/4 cup shredded cheese (~113cal/7g), 1 slice bacon (~43cal/3g) = ~860cal/67g. Dinner: half of homemade 5-cheese tortellini dish (Costco tortellini + 2 tbsp butter + 1/2 cup white wine + 1/2 cup parmesan + 3 slices bacon, half portion eaten) = ~1,260cal/60g. Final: 159g/2,820cal.

Sleep 8h. Pre-game Clif Builder Bar (~20g/270cal). Indoor basketball 50 min high intensity, achilles 1-2/10 AM / 0-1/10 during play (green per Silbernagel). 2hr medium-intensity yardwork (digging + squatting, real lower-body load). Lunch: 18-inch Italian hero + Coke (~110g/2,140cal, ~5,500-7,500mg sodium). Dinner: homemade quesadilla (2 medium Costco tortillas, 3 BLSS chicken thighs, 2/3 cup shredded cheese, 2 tbsp oil) = ~106g/1,490cal. Final: 236g/3,900cal. High-activity high-intake day.

AM weigh-in 224.9/24.1%. AM achilles 1-2/10 resolving with warmup (green per Silbernagel, 24h rule met). Breakfast: shake without granola, with yogurt (~86g/570cal). Lunch: turkey sandwich + mozzarella + 2 servings Old Thyme pretzels (~61g/810cal). Dinner: 2 slices golden wheat bread + 2 slices mozzarella + 2 BLSS chicken thighs (~70g/720cal). Extra: 1 additional BLSS chicken thigh (~25g/170cal) + 1 Its-It mini vanilla (~2g/170cal) = ~27g/340cal. Final: 244g/2,440cal. Lands at top of revised Mon anchor day target (2,300-2,500 per Option B). Restaurant meals this week: 0/3.

AM weigh-in 223.8/23.8%. AM achilles 1-2/10 (green per Silbernagel). Breakfast: default shake without granola with yogurt (~86g/590cal). Lunch: 5.5oz BLSS chicken thigh + 1 cup Ritika's cilantro lime rice (~43g/495cal). Pre-game: Clif Builder Bar (~20g/270cal). Post-game: 1.5 pints light beer (~195cal/1g) + 3 buffalo wings (~280cal/18g) + 10 french fries (~100cal/2g) = ~575cal/21g. Final: 170g/1,925cal.
